sentences.target: On traverse le tunnel en voiture.

translations.fr: On traverse le tunnel en voiture.
translations.en: We drive through the tunnel by car.
translations.de: Wir fahren mit dem Auto durch den Tunnel.
translations.ru: Мы проезжаем через тоннель на машине.
translations.vi: Chúng tôi lái xe qua đường hầm.
translations.nl: We rijden met de auto door de tunnel.
translations.hi: हम कार से सुरंग से गुजरते हैं.
translations.el: Περνάμε με το αυτοκίνητο μέσα από το τούνελ.
translations.da: Vi kører i bil gennem tunnelen.
translations.ja: 車でトンネルを通ります。

tokens.explanation: Subject pronoun (impersonal/collective); in everyday French often means ‘we’. Triggers 3rd person singular verb agreement.
Main verb, present indicative 3rd person singular; transitive, taking ‘le tunnel’ as direct object.
Definite article, masculine singular; determiner for ‘tunnel’.
Common noun, masculine; direct object of ‘traverse’.
Preposition introducing a prepositional phrase of means/manner (‘en voiture’ = by car).
Common noun, feminine; object of the preposition ‘en’ indicating means of transport.

translations.en: I'm waiting at the tunnel entrance.
translations.es: Estoy esperando en la entrada del túnel.
translations.ru: Я жду у входа в тоннель.
translations.pt: Estou à espera na entrada do túnel.
translations.vi: Tôi đang đợi ở lối vào đường hầm.
translations.nl: Ik wacht bij de ingang van de tunnel.
translations.uk: Я чекаю біля входу в тунель.
translations.hi: मैं सुरंग के प्रवेश द्वार पर इंतज़ार कर रहा हूँ.
translations.id: Saya menunggu di pintu masuk terowongan.
translations.ja: トンネルの入口で待っています。

tokens.explanation: Clitic subject pronoun elided before vowel-initial verb (‘je’ → ‘j’’).
Main verb, present indicative 1st person singular; intransitive here, taking a locative complement.
Preposition introducing a locative/goal prepositional phrase (‘à l’entrée’).
Definite article, masculine/feminine singular; elided before vowel-initial noun (‘entrée’).
Common noun, feminine; object of the preposition ‘à’.
Contracted form of ‘de’ + ‘le’; preposition introducing a genitive-like noun complement (‘du tunnel’).
Common noun, masculine; complement of ‘du’.

sentences.target: Vous évitez ce tunnel aux heures de pointe.
sentences.reading: /vu e.vi.te sə ty.nɛl o zœʁ də pwɛ̃t/

translations.fr: Vous évitez ce tunnel aux heures de pointe.
translations.ipa: /vu e.vi.te sə ty.nɛl o zœʁ də pwɛ̃t/
translations.en: You avoid this tunnel during rush hour.
translations.es: Ustedes evitan este túnel en hora punta.
translations.de: Sie meiden diesen Tunnel zu den Stoßzeiten.
translations.it: Voi evitate questo tunnel nelle ore di punta.
translations.zh-Hans: 你们在高峰时段避开这条隧道。
translations.yue: 你哋喺繁忙時間會避開呢條隧道。
translations.ru: Вы избегаете этого тоннеля в часы пик.
translations.pt: Vocês evitam este túnel nas horas de ponta.
translations.he: אתם נמנעים מהמנהרה הזו בשעות העומס.
translations.tr: Siz yoğun saatlerde bu tünelden kaçınıyorsunuz.
translations.vi: Các bạn tránh đường hầm này vào giờ cao điểm.
translations.ar: أنتم تتجنبون هذا النفق في ساعات الذروة.
translations.nl: Jullie vermijden deze tunnel tijdens de spits.
translations.uk: Ви уникаєте цього тунелю в години пік.
translations.hu: Önök csúcsidőben elkerülik ezt az alagutat.
translations.hi: आप लोग भीड़भाड़ के समय इस सुरंग से बचते हैं.
translations.pl: Unikacie tego tunelu w godzinach szczytu.
translations.el: Αποφεύγετε αυτό το τούνελ στις ώρες αιχμής.
translations.nb: Dere unngår denne tunnelen i rushtiden.
translations.id: Anda menghindari terowongan ini pada jam sibuk.
translations.sv: Ni undviker den här tunneln under rusningstid.
translations.ro: Evitați acest tunel la orele de vârf.
translations.cs: Vyhýbáte se tomuto tunelu v době špičky.
translations.da: I undgår denne tunnel i myldretiden.
translations.ja: ラッシュ時にはこのトンネルを避けます。

tokens.explanation: Personal pronoun; subject, 2nd person plural (or polite singular).
Main verb, present indicative 2nd person plural; transitive, taking ‘ce tunnel’ as direct object.
Demonstrative determiner, masculine singular; modifies ‘tunnel’.
Common noun, masculine; direct object of ‘évitez’.
Contracted form of ‘à’ + ‘les’; preposition introducing a temporal phrase (‘aux heures de pointe’).
Common noun, feminine plural; head of the temporal prepositional phrase.
Preposition introducing the noun complement (‘de pointe’) specifying the type of hours.
Common noun, feminine; complement of ‘de’ in the fixed expression ‘heures de pointe’ (rush hours).

sentences.target: Le GPS nous fait passer par un tunnel.
sentences.reading: /lə ʒe.pe.ɛs nu fɛ pa.se paʁ œ̃ ty.nɛl/

translations.fr: Le GPS nous fait passer par un tunnel.
translations.ipa: /lə ʒe.pe.ɛs nu fɛ pa.se paʁ œ̃ ty.nɛl/
translations.en: The GPS makes us go through a tunnel.
translations.es: El GPS nos lleva por un túnel.
translations.de: Das GPS führt uns durch einen Tunnel.
translations.it: Il GPS ci fa passare per un tunnel.
translations.pt: O GPS leva-nos por um túnel.
translations.vi: GPS dẫn chúng tôi đi qua một đường hầm.
translations.ar: نظام تحديد المواقع يوجهنا عبر نفق.
translations.nl: De gps stuurt ons durch een tunnel.
translations.hu: A GPS egy alagúton keresztül vezet minket.
translations.hi: जीपीएस हमें एक सुरंग से होकर ले जाता है.
translations.el: Το GPS μάς οδηγεί μέσα από ένα τούνελ.
translations.nb: GPS-en leder oss gjennom en tunnel.
translations.id: GPS mengarahkan kami melalui sebuah terowongan.
translations.sv: GPS:en leder oss genom en tunnel.
translations.da: GPS'en leder os gennem en tunnel.
translations.ja: GPSが私たちをトンネル経由で案内します。

tokens.explanation: Definite article, masculine singular; determiner for ‘GPS’.
Proper noun (acronym); subject of the clause.
Clitic object pronoun; the causee/patient in the causative construction with ‘fait passer’.
Main verb, present indicative 3rd person singular; introduces causative construction with infinitive ‘passer’.
Infinitive verb (xcomp of ‘fait’) expressing the action caused.
Preposition introducing the route/path (‘par un tunnel’).
Indefinite article, masculine singular; determiner for ‘tunnel’.
Common noun, masculine; object of the preposition ‘par’.
